title: echinochloa crus-galli subsp. submutica
intro: Echinochloa crus-galli subsp. submutica, also known as Japanese barnyard grass or wild rice, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Echinochloa crus-galli subsp. submutica include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antiseptic, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The plant is often prepared for medicinal use by making tea from its leaves and stems, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal dosage.
